name: Processed cheese (food medium)
basis: per 100 g (pasteurized process American cheese; USDA FDC 171289, vitamin-D-fortified)

components.name: water
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 (SR Legacy pasteurized process American cheese)
components.evidence: Moisture of process American cheese ~39 g/100g; remainder is fat 31 + protein 18 + carb 5.3 + ash ~5 (very salty).

components.name: lactose
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 total sugars 2.6 g; Ahmed et al. 2023 Food Sci Nutr doi:10.1002/fsn3.2438 (cheese HPLC)
components.evidence: Total sugars 2.6 g/100g (USDA); in process cheese most of this is lactose introduced by added whey/milk solids (natural cheese matrix is nearly lactose-free). Amount = total sugars minus measured galactose. Dominant fermentable sugar.

components.name: D-galactose
components.source: Ahmed et al. 2023 doi:10.1002/fsn3.2438 (Cheddar HPLC, 0.27% w/w)
components.evidence: Free galactose accumulates from starter-bacteria lactose hydrolysis; measured 0.27% in Cheddar, the closest measured matrix to American process cheese.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Not detected in any cheese variety by HPLC; present at most in trace. Included for completeness.

components.name: lactic acid
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid and a primary carbon/energy source; from starter fermentation of lactose. Process cheese may add extra lactic acid as acidulant, so this is a lower bound.

components.name: acetic acid
components.evidence: Second major short-chain acid from heterofermentative and citrate metabolism.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Native milk citrate ~0.056%. NOTE: process cheese commonly uses sodium citrate as an emulsifying salt, which can raise total citrate well above this native value (product-dependent).

components.name: propionic acid
components.evidence: From propionibacteria / secondary flora; measurable in aged cheese blends used for process cheese.

components.name: pyruvic acid
components.evidence: Central metabolic intermediate detected by HPLC.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: TCA-derived acid, minor.

components.name: orotic acid
components.evidence: Milk-derived pyrimidine precursor, characteristic of dairy.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Casein-dominated; the amino-acid entries below are the directly measured USDA panel for American cheese (FDC 171252, protein 16.8 g) and partition essentially the same casein protein.

components.name: L-glutamic acid
components.source: USDA FDC 171252 (American cheese, direct measured AA panel)
components.evidence: Most abundant residue in casein (incl. glutamine); major N and secondary C source; umami driver.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: Casein-rich residue; abundant free proline in ripened cheese.

components.name: L-leucine
components.evidence: Branched-chain essential.

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: Essential AA.

components.name: L-aspartic acid
components.evidence: Incl. asparagine.

components.name: L-valine
components.evidence: Branched-chain essential; MISSING from current FooDB list, added.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Replaces the FooDB 'D-Serine' entry (wrong stereoisomer for a food protein).

components.name: L-isoleucine
components.evidence: Branched-chain essential.

components.name: L-tyrosine
components.evidence: Aromatic.

components.name: L-phenylalanine
components.evidence: Aromatic essential; MISSING from current FooDB list, added.

components.name: L-threonine
components.evidence: Essential.

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Basic residue.

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Sulfur AA.

components.name: L-histidine
components.evidence: Essential.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Essential.

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Sulfur AA (cysteine dimer).

components.name: Sodium
components.evidence: Very high: added NaCl plus sodium-phosphate/citrate emulsifying salts. Defining feature of process cheese.

components.name: Calcium
components.evidence: Casein-bound + emulsifier salts.

components.name: Phosphate (as P)
components.evidence: High; native casein phosphate plus added phosphate emulsifiers.

components.name: Iron
components.evidence: Consolidates the three redundant FooDB iron entries ('Iron', 'Iron (Fe3+)', 'Fe2+ mitochondria').

components.name: Copper
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: Manganese
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: Selenium
components.evidence: USDA-range ~14-20 ug/100g for process American cheese.

components.name: Chloride
components.source: inferred from Sodium 1660 mg (NaCl + emulsifier salts)
components.evidence: Counter-ion of the salt load; abundant but not separately tabulated by USDA. Present, ~2000-2500 mg estimated from salt content.

components.name: Ammonium
components.evidence: Free ammonia/ammonium from proteolysis during ripening; real but low, not quantified.

components.name: Vitamin A (RAE, retinol)
components.evidence: Predominantly preformed retinol from milkfat.

components.name: Pantothenic acid (B5)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: Niacin (B3)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: Thiamine (B1)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: Folate (B9)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: Choline
components.source: USDA (processed American cheese) via nutrientoptimiser USDA render
components.evidence: Covers the FooDB 'Choline' and 'Glycine betaine' entries (betaine is the choline oxidation product, present in dairy).

components.name: Vitamin D (D3)
components.evidence: This value reflects the FORTIFIED product; unfortified process cheese is ~0.4-0.6 ug/100g.

components.name: Vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Milkfat-associated. Also small gamma-tocopherol present.

components.name: Vitamin K1 (phylloquinone)
components.evidence: Dairy also carries K2 menaquinones (MK-4/MK-8/MK-9); MK-4 (menatetrenone) kept in uncovered.

components.name: Vitamin C (ascorbate)
components.evidence: Cheese is essentially devoid of vitamin C; the FooDB 'L-Ascorbate' entry is negligible/trace.

components.name: palmitic acid (C16:0)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render
components.evidence: Dominant saturated fatty acid of milkfat.

components.name: oleic acid (C18:1)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render
components.evidence: Dominant monounsaturated FA (FooDB '(Z)-octadecenoic' family).

components.name: stearic acid (C18:0)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: myristic acid (C14:0)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: linoleic acid (C18:2)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render
components.evidence: FooDB '(Z,Z)-9,12-octadecadienoic acid'.

components.name: lauric acid (C12:0, dodecanoic)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: capric acid (C10:0, decanoic)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: butyric acid (C4:0)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render
components.evidence: Characteristic short-chain milkfat acid; partly present free (flavor/carbon source).

components.name: caproic acid (C6:0, hexanoic)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: caprylic acid (C8:0, octanoic)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (C18:3 n-3)
components.source: USDA FDC 171289 via logixpathchef USDA render
components.evidence: Ruminant-derived omega-3, minor.

components.name: pentadecanoic acid (C15:0)
components.source: USDA milkfat profile (odd-chain marker); FooDB entry
components.evidence: Odd-chain dairy biomarker, ~0.3 g/100g in milkfat; genuine, kept.

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: Genuine animal sterol (cheese is an animal food) - retained, not FooDB noise.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a growth nutrient of cheese; FooDB predicted.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: free cobalt ion is a FooDB trace/predicted artifact; cobalt is relevant only as the B12 core, which is already captured by cyanocobalamin.

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: negligible/trace in dairy, FooDB predicted; not a defining growth nutrient.

remove.name: Fluoride
remove.reason: trace, non-nutritive; not a growth-relevant nutrient of cheese.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: wrong stereoisomer - food protein contains L-serine, not D-serine; replaced by L-serine.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: redundant duplicate iron entry; consolidated into a single 'Iron' component (0.48 mg).

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: FooDB compartment-tagged duplicate of iron; consolidated into 'Iron'.

remove.name: Gamolenic acid
remove.reason: gamma-linolenic acid is not a genuine constituent of ruminant milkfat; FooDB predicted/implausible.

remove.name: Eicosapentaenoic acid
remove.reason: EPA is at best a trace in dairy fat; the listed presence overstates it - FooDB predicted, drop as noise.

remove.name: Doconexent
remove.reason: DHA is a trace-only ruminant-milk component; FooDB predicted, drop as noise.

remove.name: Starch C12H20O10
remove.reason: standard pasteurized process American cheese contains no added starch; FooDB predicted (relevant only to specific starch-stabilized 'cheese product' variants).

missing_added: lactic acid (1.44 g/100g - DOMINANT organic acid, was absent)
citric acid (native ~0.06 g; note sodium-citrate emulsifier can raise it)
quantitative individual sugars (lactose, galactose) with amounts
full quantitative amino-acid panel (all 18 residues from the directly measured USDA American-cheese panel)
niacin, thiamine, folate, choline, copper, manganese quantities
keep_uncovered: Menatetrenone (vitamin K2 MK-4 - genuine dairy menaquinone, no distinct BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 171289 (Cheese product, pasteurized process, American, vitamin D fortified) - macros, minerals, vitamins, fatty acids, cholesterol
USDA FoodData Central FDC 171252 (American cheese) - full directly-measured 18-residue amino-acid panel

Ahmed et al. 2023, Food Science & Nutrition, doi:10.1002/fsn3.2438, PMID 37181312 (HPLC carbohydrates and organic acids in cheese varieties) - lactose, galactose, lactic/acetic/citric/propionic/pyruvic/succinic/orotic acids
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe growing on process cheese are LACTIC ACID (~1.44 g/100g, previously missing) and LACTOSE (~2.3 g/100g from added dairy solids), followed by acetate; free amino acids / casein-derived glutamate+proline provide N and additional C, and milkfat fatty acids (palmitate, oleate, plus free short-chain C4-C10) are a lipid substrate. The matrix is extremely sodium-rich (emulsifying salts) and citrate can be elevated where sodium-citrate emulsifier is used. Macros, minerals, vitamins, fatty acids and cholesterol are direct USDA values (FDC 171289); the complete amino-acid panel is the directly measured USDA American-cheese panel (FDC 171252); organic acids and the lactose/galactose split are from peer-reviewed HPLC (Ahmed 2023) using Cheddar as the closest-matrix process-cheese base. concentration_mM assumes 100 g food made up to 1 L of medium.
